title: rosa obtusifolia var. stantiensis
intro: Rosa obtusifolia var. stantiensis, also known as the wild rose, is a perennial plant species native to China, characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Rosa obtusifolia var. stantiensis are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic effects. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
